page: Amazon Web Services r8id.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 128 virtual CPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 1024.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

This instance includes 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering high-capacity, low-latency local storage. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. While it lacks dedicated GPU hardware, the Intel Xeon processors support advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate matrix-math operations for machine learning and cryptographic workloads.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8id.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for multi-core CPU stress tests, Passmark CPU scores, and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read bandwidths of up to 10196.19 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 384.54 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, single-core performance is more moderate, placing in the middle 50% for single-core stress tests. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top 10%, highlighting its efficiency in handling high-throughput data transactions.

Given its high density of DDR5 memory, massive local NVMe storage, and dedicated compute resources, the r8id.32xlarge offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise workloads that require high resource density without the overhead of separate storage networks. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching layers, high-throughput data processing, and LLM inference tasks utilizing CPU-based matrix acceleration.
description: The Amazon Web Services r8id.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors with 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) running at 3.9 GHz. It features 1024.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The CPU architecture supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions, enabling accelerated matrix and cryptographic operations. Benchmark data shows top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and memory read performance, though single-core performance remains average. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads, such as large-scale databases, Redis caching, and CPU-based machine learning inference.

tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized platform featuring massive local NVMe storage and advanced matrix acceleration for demanding database and data processing workloads.

1024 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix and cryptographic calculations.

Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching, and data-intensive applications.
